identity.short_name: GCD \to discriminant \to digit sum \to engineered (2γ+1) derivative \to splitting field \to log modulus

problem_spec.summary: Compute F=gcd(P,Q) for two integer polynomials, take the discriminant δ of the (quadratic) gcd and its digit sum γ. Over F_p, define an engineered polynomial f(x)=x^{pδ}+c x^{2γ+1}-x where c is the inverse of (2γ+1) in F_p. In characteristic p the derivative kills x^{pδ} and the choice of c forces f'(x)=x^{2γ}-1. Determine the smallest finite field extension where x^{2γ}-1 splits using multiplicative order, evaluate log_{p^2}(m), and compute δ modulo 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)+γ.
givens: P(x), Q(x) are degree-6 polynomials in ℤ[x].
F(x)=gcd(P(x),Q(x)) in ℤ[x], fixed by requiring positive leading coefficient.
F(x) is quadratic: F(x)=ax^2+bx+c with a>0.
γ = sum of decimal digits of δ.
p is a prime (this instance uses p=29).
Work in _p and define c = (2γ+1)^{-1} \in _p (assumed to exist).
Define f(x)=x^{pδ}+c x^{2γ+1}-x in _p[x] and let f'(x) be its formal derivative.
m is the smallest prime power such that f'(x) splits completely into linear factors over _m.
ask: Compute output = δ mod (2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)+γ), with the residue taken in {0,...,2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)+γ-1}.

parameter(s).name: P_coeffs
parameter(s).domain: integer coefficient list defining a degree-6 polynomial in ℤ[x]
parameter(s).purpose: determines the gcd F and hence δ and γ

parameter(s).name: Q_coeffs
parameter(s).domain: integer coefficient list defining a degree-6 polynomial in ℤ[x]
parameter(s).purpose: determines the gcd F and hence δ and γ

parameter(s).name: base_prime_p
parameter(s).purpose: sets characteristic for the formal derivative and determines finite-field sizes p^d

parameter(s).name: exponent_template
parameter(s).domain: f(x)=x^{pδ}+c x^{2γ+1}-x with c=(2γ+1)^{-1} in F_p
parameter(s).purpose: forces derivative cancellation of the pδ term and normalizes the x^{2γ} coefficient to 1

parameter(s).name: log_base
parameter(s).purpose: fixes the logarithm base so log_{p^2}(p^d)=d/2 and 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d is an integer

invariant(s).name: gcd_uniqueness_in_Zx
invariant(s).statement: F(x)=gcd(P,Q) is uniquely determined up to ±1, fixed by requiring positive leading coefficient.

invariant(s).name: discriminant_is_integer
invariant(s).statement: For quadratic F(x)=ax^2+bx+c in ℤ[x], δ=b^2-4ac is an integer determined by coefficients.

invariant(s).name: derivative_cancellation_in_char_p
invariant(s).statement: In characteristic p, (x^{pδ})' = pδ \cdot x^{pδ-1} = 0, so the x^{pδ} term vanishes in f'(x).

invariant(s).name: engineered_inverse_for_unit_x_2gamma_term
invariant(s).statement: With c=(2γ+1)^{-1} in _p, we have (c x^{2γ+1})' = c(2γ+1)x^{2γ} = x^{2γ}, forcing f'(x)=x^{2γ}-1.

invariant(s).name: splitting_of_xn_minus_1
invariant(s).statement: If gcd(p,2γ)=1, then x^{2γ}-1 splits over _{p^d} iff 2γ | (p^d-1). The minimal such d is ord_{2γ}(p).

invariant(s).name: log_p2_prime_power
invariant(s).statement: If m=p^d then log_{p^2}(m)=d/2, hence 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d is an integer.

essential_assumption(s): The computed gcd F(x) has degree 2 (quadratic).
2γ+1 is nonzero in _p (equivalently p ∤ (2γ+1)), so c=(2γ+1)^{-1} exists.
gcd(p,2γ)=1 so x^{2γ}-1 has distinct roots and the roots-of-unity splitting criterion applies cleanly.
m is taken among finite fields _{p^d} (prime-power sizes).
uniqueness_certificate(s): F is uniquely determined as the gcd in ℤ[x] with positive leading coefficient, hence δ and γ are deterministic. The coefficient c=(2γ+1)^{-1} is unique in _p, so f' is uniquely x^{2γ}-1. The smallest extension degree d for splitting is uniquely ord_{2γ}(p), hence m=p^d is unique. Since 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d and γ are fixed, the modulus (d+γ) and the residue δ mod (d+γ) are uniquely determined.

technique(s).name: compute_gcd_by_structured_difference
technique(s).purpose: compute F=gcd(P,Q) using that gcd divides P-Q and a visible common quadratic factor

technique(s).name: digit_sum_evaluation
technique(s).purpose: compute γ as the sum of decimal digits of δ

technique(s).name: invert_scalar_in_Fp
technique(s).purpose: compute c=(2γ+1)^{-1} to normalize the derivative coefficient

technique(s).name: formal_derivative_engineering_to_x_2gamma_minus_1
technique(s).purpose: use characteristic-p cancellation and inverse normalization to force f'(x)=x^{2γ}-1

technique(s).name: minimal_splitting_field_for_xn_minus_1
technique(s).purpose: find minimal d such that n | (p^d-1), i.e. d=ord_n(p), so m=p^d

technique(s).name: evaluate_logarithm_of_prime_power_and_reduce_modulus
technique(s).purpose: compute 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d and then reduce δ modulo d+γ

theorem(s).name: Euclidean_algorithm_in_Zx
theorem(s).role: defines and guarantees uniqueness of gcd up to units in ℤ[x]

theorem(s).name: formal_derivative_in_characteristic_p
theorem(s).role: (x^{pk})' = 0 and (x^n)' = n x^{n-1} with coefficients reduced mod p

theorem(s).name: roots_of_unity_splitting_criterion
theorem(s).role: x^n-1 splits over _q iff n | (q-1) when gcd(n,char)=1

theorem(s).name: multiplicative_order_mod_n
theorem(s).role: minimal d with p^d \equiv 1 (mod n) equals ord_n(p)

critical_trick(s): Use P-Q to isolate the quadratic gcd immediately.
Engineer f so that the pδ-exponent term vanishes under differentiation and the x^{2γ} coefficient becomes 1 via c=(2γ+1)^{-1}.
Reduce splitting to an order computation ord_{2γ}(p) and turn 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m) into the integer d.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: exact gcd/discriminant computation and finite-field coefficient reduction for verification

import_hint(s): from sympy import symbols, Poly, gcd, discriminant, GF

task(s).name: verify_gcd_and_discriminant
task(s).reason: compute gcd(P,Q) and discriminant(F) exactly in ℤ[x] as an audit

task(s).name: verify_derivative_engineering
task(s).reason: compute f'(x) in GF(p) and confirm it equals x^{2γ}-1

task(s).name: verify_minimal_order
task(s).reason: compute ord_{2γ}(p) and confirm n | (p^d-1) holds first at that d
